element_introduction_order.mastery_context: sufficient for weak enemies; establishes player competency baseline

enemies moving/attacking faster on high difficulty
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Labyrinthine exploration interspersed with combat encounters. Tension builds through enemy density escalation across episodes. Relief cycles generated by weapon/item discovery and safe room transitions. Boss fights serve as climactic punctuation marks ending each episode.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: side branches and alcoves often contain weapon upgrades or power-ups; optional exploration rewarded

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate. Introduces pistol, shotgun, basic enemy types (undead, demons) in manageable group sizes. Establishes weapon-switching and navigation fundamentals. Keycard-gating teaches progression pacing.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating. Enemy group sizes increase; tougher demon types appear. Player has access to plasma rifle and advanced power-ups. Environmental hazards (toxic waste, crushing ceilings) become more prevalent. Boss encounters increase in complexity.
difficulty_curve.late_game: High intensity. Dense enemy encounters; BFG 9000 available for power fantasy moments. Final episode pushes to Hell setting; culminates in climactic boss fight. Difficulty settings affect enemy speed and damage output significantly.
notable_spikes: Episode 3 Hell setting (increased demon types and density)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player navigates complex, interconnected spaces to locate marked exit room. Non-linear routing creates sense of discovery. Multiple paths encourage backtracking and environmental mastery.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic to all levels in Episodes 1-3

notable_level_patterns.description: Final level of each episode culminates in single-boss or mini-boss fight. Serves as episode climax and difficulty spike. Requires weapon management and arena awareness.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Knee-Deep in the Dead finale, Shores of Hell finale, Inferno finale

notable_level_patterns.description: Locked doors require colored keycards or remote switches collected during exploration. Creates mandatory routing and discourages rushing. Reinforces spatial memory.
Environmental dangers force spatial problem-solving. Toxic waste pits require radiation suits or platforming skill. Crushing ceilings add time-pressure to decision-making.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Referenced in 'Knee-Deep in the Dead' level descriptions

sources_used: wikipedia - Doom (1993 video game) - Gameplay section
